expectations.text: Document now contains all required sections including the previously missing Detection Opportunities, Data Sources & Log Fields, and Pseudocode Queries
expectations.evidence: All required sections are present: 'Technique Overview' (line 5), 'Attack Steps' (line 31), 'Detection Opportunities' (line 76), 'Data Sources & Log Fields' (line 107), 'Pseudocode Queries' (line 175), 'MITRE ATT&CK Mapping' (line 264). All three previously missing sections (Detection Opportunities, Data Sources & Log Fields, Pseudocode Queries) are now fully populated.

expectations.text: MITRE mapping now uses T1071.004 (DNS sub-technique) instead of just T1071
expectations.evidence: MITRE ATT&CK Mapping table at line 268 explicitly lists 'T1071.004 | Application Layer Protocol: DNS', not the parent T1071. This is the specific DNS sub-technique as required.

expectations.text: At least one detection method is labeled with High, Medium, or Low fidelity
expectations.evidence: Detection Opportunities section contains multiple fidelity-labeled entries: 'High Volume of DNS Queries to a Single Domain (High Fidelity)' (line 78), 'High-Entropy Subdomain Labels (High Fidelity)' (line 80), 'System.Net.Dns API Usage Without Child Process (Medium Fidelity)' (line 84). Pseudocode queries also carry fidelity headings (lines 177, 189, 198, 208, 216, 224, 232).

expectations.text: Attack Steps section has more than the original 3 bullet points, ideally with PowerShell code examples
expectations.evidence: The Attack Steps section contains 7 numbered steps (lines 33-72), well exceeding 3. Steps include PowerShell code examples: a full 'Encode-DnsLabel' and 'Send-DnsBeacon' function at lines 41-55, and Python pseudocode for the server side at lines 64-67.

expectations.text: A Pseudocode Queries section exists and contains at least one detection query
expectations.evidence: The 'Pseudocode Queries' section (line 175) contains 6 distinct detection queries, including 'High Fidelity: PowerShell DNS API Call with Encoded Subdomain' (line 177), 'High Fidelity: High-Entropy DNS Subdomain from PowerShell' (line 189), and additional Medium fidelity and Behavioral queries through line 260.

suggestions: The behavioral beacon detection query (lines 253-260) uses a custom 'statistical_beacon_score()' function that may not be directly portable — consider adding a simpler aggregate alternative as a fallback.
Could add explicit 'Low fidelity' labeled detections to complete the fidelity spectrum.
eval_feedback.overall: Excellent output. All 5 assertions pass. The document is comprehensive, well-structured, and correctly uses T1071.004. Fidelity labels are applied consistently throughout Detection Opportunities and Pseudocode Queries. Attack Steps include rich PowerShell examples.
